install.WARNING: npm install is DEPRECATED. Do NOT use 'npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code'. Use one of the methods above.
install.note: After install, first-time setup MUST add allowedTools to settings for non-interactive use. See first_run below.

first_run.description: Claude Code requires TWO config files for non-interactive use: (1) config.json to skip login/onboarding, (2) settings.json for tool permissions. Without these, Claude Code will hang waiting for interactive prompts.

config_json.description: MUST be created FIRST. Skips login screen and onboarding wizard.

settings_json.description: Tool permissions. Without allowedTools, Claude Code prompts for folder trust and tool approvals interactively.

first_run.setup_script:
python3 -c "
import json, os
# Step 1: config.json - skip onboarding/login
cpath = os.path.expanduser('~/.claude/config.json')
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(cpath), exist_ok=True)
cc = {}
if os.path.exists(cpath):
    with open(cpath) as f: cc = json.load(f)
cc['hasCompletedOnboarding'] = True
cc['primaryApiKey'] = 'dummy'
with open(cpath, 'w') as f: json.dump(cc, f, indent=2)
print('config.json: onboarding skipped')
# Step 2: settings.json - tool permissions
spath = os.path.expanduser('~/.claude/settings.json')
sc = {}
if os.path.exists(spath):
    with open(spath) as f: sc = json.load(f)
if 'allowedTools' not in sc:
    sc['allowedTools'] = ['Edit','Write','Bash','Read','MultiEdit','Glob','Grep','LS','TodoRead','TodoWrite','WebFetch','NotebookRead','NotebookEdit']
with open(spath, 'w') as f: json.dump(sc, f, indent=2)
print('settings.json: allowedTools set')
"
